data.description:
PLACEHOLDER EPIC (P0) — to be brainstormed/decomposed before execution.

Goal: Augment the existing brainstorm/plan review process (epic-scrutiny-pipeline,
red-team-reviewer, plan-review) with the code-grounded adversarial review techniques
that proved high-signal during the MQ-rollback / Option A pivot session (2026-06-03).
Those techniques repeatedly caught load-bearing flaws that prose-level review missed
(e.g. a CI "parity safety net" that does not actually exist; an allowlist edit that
silently misses a hardcoded duplicate; "mirror this exemption" work that was actually
greenfield in 3 scripts).

Techniques to fold in (each became a finding axis this session):
1. Code-grounded citation requirement — every load-bearing claim in a plan/ADR must
   cite file:line the reviewer actually OPENED; a finding with no code citation is
   itself a review defect. (No reasoning from plan prose alone.)
2. FILENAME-ASSUMPTION axis — explicitly flag claims that rest on a filename, function
   name, or "it probably works that way" rather than read code; require the reviewer to
   open the source and confirm the behavior.
3. UNVERIFIED-ASSUMPTION axis — separate "asserted PASS/works/safe" from
   "experimentally run against the live system"; emit a checklist of named experiments
   with the exact command/action that would settle each (the ADR E-series/V-series
   pattern).
4. MISSING-CONSUMER axis — broad grep/ast-grep sweep for callers, dependents, and
   duplicated logic the plan's inventory omits (hardcoded duplicates, untracked
   scripts/skills/docs/tests/hooks).
5. LOGIC/SEQUENCING-GAP axis — order-of-operations hazards (enforce-flip before the
   fail-closed hole is fixed; add a required check before the workflow reliably reports
   it; carve-outs that can wedge or launder; migration/transition hazards).
6. Iterated multi-pass adversarial review — sequential passes, each told the easy
   findings are gone and the remaining flaws are subtle (bias-to-skepticism prompt).
7. Live-system verification DURING review — reviewer runs read-only API/CLI queries
   (e.g. gh api ruleset reads) to check plan claims about live config against reality.
8. "Claims verified as SOUND (do not re-litigate)" output section — so subsequent
   passes don't re-burn budget on settled claims.

Scope notes / candidate integration points (to confirm in brainstorm):
- plugins/dso/skills/shared/workflows/epic-scrutiny-pipeline.md
- dso:red-team-reviewer, dso:plan-review agents and their prompts
- brainstorm SKILL.md scrutiny gates (scrutiny:pending / ui_probes:deferred)
- the RESEARCH-PATTERN.md / prior-art-search guidance

Reference artifact from this session: /tmp/option-a-review4-findings.md (review #4
output) and docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md §2.6–§2.10 review-pass history.

Acceptance (to be refined): the brainstorm/plan review process produces, for any
high-blast-radius plan, a structured report carrying the axes above with file:line
evidence and an experiments-required checklist — reproducing the signal demonstrated
this session.
data.title: improve plan review with additional code grounding techniques
